title: McIlroy's Back-to-Back Brilliance: Masters 2026 Recapped
excerpt: Rory McIlroy captured his second consecutive Masters title, edging Scottie Scheffler by one shot to claim his sixth major championship and join an elite quartet of back-to-back winners.
body:
McIlroy Claims Second Green Jacket

Rory McIlroy etched his name into Augusta National folklore on Sunday, winning the 2026 Masters Tournament to capture his second consecutive green jacket. The Northern Irishman's 12-under-par victory over Scottie Scheffler on a drama-filled final day extended one of golf's most compelling narratives — a redemptive journey that culminated in joining Jack Nicklaus, Nick Faldo and Tiger Woods as only the fourth player in Masters history to win back-to-back titles.

Winning Score
-12 (276)
Victory Margin
1 Shot
McIlroy Major Titles
6
Winner's Prize
$4.5m

The Chase and the Win

McIlroy held a share of the lead after every single round — a testament to the relentless precision demanded by Augusta's pristine greens and lightning-fast bentgrass. His closing 71 proved good enough as Scheffler, the pre-tournament favourite at 5/1 odds, shot a bogey-free 68 to finish at 11-under, one agonising stroke back. For much of Sunday, it appeared Scheffler's world-class ball-striking would prevail, but McIlroy's experience and ice-cold resolve carried the day.

The Scheffler Contingent

Scottie Scheffler ran out of miracles on the final nine holes. Despite being the dominant force in world golf for two years, Scheffler could not find that final birdie to force a play-off. He finished second, ahead of a five-way tie for third that featured Cameron Young, Justin Rose, Tyrrell Hatton, Russell Henley and Sam Burns, all at 10-under par. Young's runner-up finishes keep stacking; Rose and Hatton proved Augusta's elegance suits their methodical, patient games.

The Narrative Complete

McIlroy's 2025 Masters victory exorcised a decade-long demon. For years, his Augusta story was one of near-misses and Sunday collapses. That changed last April when he broke through to win his first green jacket, completing the career Grand Slam. Returning this year as defending champion, McIlroy played with the freedom of a man with nothing left to prove. He arrived loose, liberated, and absolutely dangerous — and it showed.

What's Next

The PGA Tour transitions to Harbour Town this week for the RBC Heritage, with McIlroy taking a step back from competition. The next major isn't until May 14-17 at Aronimink Golf Club in Philadelphia for the PGA Championship. McIlroy's sixth major ties him with legends like Gary Player and five-time major winners including Tom Watson. The pursuit of Jack Nicklaus's record eighteen continues.
